verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. It lacks a transmembrane domain and has no outer-leaflet lipidation, GPI anchor, or secretory isoform. Canonically it resides on the inner leaflet and ER/Golgi, operating as an intracellular signaling kinase in BCR/FcεRI pathways. While cancer cells may display ecto-kinase activity in some contexts, baseline LYN is not surface-accessible from the extracellular face.
